urgent_methodology: Publisher-marked Breaking, Urgent, Alert, Just in, Developing or similar items are collected first and receive priority over standard items, unless they are liveblogs, programme previews or low-value content.

latest.title: Five arrested after Hong Kong police raid independent bookshops
latest.summary: Five people were arrested in Hong Kong for selling books deemed 'seditious' by authorities. Police raided two independent bookshops, seizing publications that allegedly incited hatred against the government, judiciary, and law enforcement.
latest.details: Five people were arrested in Hong Kong for selling books deemed 'seditious' by authorities. Police raided two independent bookshops, seizing publications that allegedly incited hatred against the government, judiciary, and law enforcement. The arrested individuals, aged between 30 and 59, are accused of violating national security laws and could face up to seven years in prison. Amnesty International criticized the raids, stating they reflect a climate of fear and self-censorship in Hong Kong.
latest.full_brief: Five people were arrested in Hong Kong for selling books deemed 'seditious' by authorities. Police raided two independent bookshops, seizing publications that allegedly incited hatred against the government, judiciary, and law enforcement. The arrested individuals, aged between 30 and 59, are accused of violating national security laws and could face up to seven years in prison. Amnesty International criticized the raids, stating they reflect a climate of fear and self-censorship in Hong Kong.

latest.title: Sierra Leone drops treason charge against ex-president over attempted coup
latest.summary: Sierra Leone's government has dropped treason charges against ex-President Ernest Bai Koroma, who was accused of involvement in a 2023 attempted coup. Information Minister Chernor Bah stated the charges were dropped on health grounds, allowing Koroma to return to Sierra Leone at his discretion.
latest.details: Sierra Leone's government has dropped treason charges against ex-President Ernest Bai Koroma, who was accused of involvement in a 2023 attempted coup. Information Minister Chernor Bah stated the charges were dropped on health grounds, allowing Koroma to return to Sierra Leone at his discretion. Koroma expressed gratitude to President Julius Maada Bio, Nigerian President Bola Tinubu, and Ecowas for their support during the process. The attempted coup in November 2023 resulted in 20 deaths and the release of nearly 2,000 inmates, with 35 individuals later convicted and sentenced to long prison terms.
latest.full_brief: Sierra Leone's government has dropped treason charges against ex-President Ernest Bai Koroma, who was accused of involvement in a 2023 attempted coup. Information Minister Chernor Bah stated the charges were dropped on health grounds, allowing Koroma to return to Sierra Leone at his discretion. Koroma expressed gratitude to President Julius Maada Bio, Nigerian President Bola Tinubu, and Ecowas for their support during the process. The attempted coup in November 2023 resulted in 20 deaths and the release of nearly 2,000 inmates, with 35 individuals later convicted and sentenced to long prison terms.

latest.title: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
latest.summary: An Israeli airstrike on a Hamas-run police post in northern Gaza killed at least seven people, including a senior officer and a woman, according to health and police officials. The Israeli military stated that the strike targeted Col Mohammed Marwan Salem, head of military security for Hamas's Central Jabalia Battalion, and three other individuals identified as terrorists.
latest.details: An Israeli airstrike on a Hamas-run police post in northern Gaza killed at least seven people, including a senior officer and a woman, according to health and police officials. The Israeli military stated that the strike targeted Col Mohammed Marwan Salem, head of military security for Hamas's Central Jabalia Battalion, and three other individuals identified as terrorists. In separate incidents, two more people, including a 10-year-old boy, were reportedly killed by Israeli fire in southern Gaza. Both Israel and Hamas have accused each other of violating the ceasefire established in October 2023, with over 1,110 Palestinians and four Israeli soldiers killed since then.
latest.full_brief: An Israeli airstrike on a Hamas-run police post in northern Gaza killed at least seven people, including a senior officer and a woman, according to health and police officials. The Israeli military stated that the strike targeted Col Mohammed Marwan Salem, head of military security for Hamas's Central Jabalia Battalion, and three other individuals identified as terrorists. In separate incidents, two more people, including a 10-year-old boy, were reportedly killed by Israeli fire in southern Gaza. Both Israel and Hamas have accused each other of violating the ceasefire established in October 2023, with over 1,110 Palestinians and four Israeli soldiers killed since then.

latest.title: 'People don't realise how at risk they are': A day with an ambulance service in a heatwave
latest.summary: Paramedics in South West England are responding to a surge in heatwave-related emergencies, including cases of heat exhaustion and dehydration. The South Western Ambulance Service received over 4,000 emergency 999 calls daily during the June heatwave, surpassing typical winter peaks of 3,000 calls.
latest.details: Paramedics in South West England are responding to a surge in heatwave-related emergencies, including cases of heat exhaustion and dehydration. The South Western Ambulance Service received over 4,000 emergency 999 calls daily during the June heatwave, surpassing typical winter peaks of 3,000 calls. Vulnerable groups, such as the elderly and those with chronic conditions, are at higher risk due to their reduced ability to regulate body temperature. The service also anticipates additional pressure from water-related injuries and drownings, which tend to increase during hot weather.
latest.full_brief: Paramedics in South West England are responding to a surge in heatwave-related emergencies, including cases of heat exhaustion and dehydration. The South Western Ambulance Service received over 4,000 emergency 999 calls daily during the June heatwave, surpassing typical winter peaks of 3,000 calls. Vulnerable groups, such as the elderly and those with chronic conditions, are at higher risk due to their reduced ability to regulate body temperature. The service also anticipates additional pressure from water-related injuries and drownings, which tend to increase during hot weather.

latest.title: Australia news live: Josh Thomas weighs in on Divine Playhouse debate; meth surges in workplace…
latest.summary: More Australian workers are taking methamphetamine on the job, according to a report by the Drug Detection Agency. Amphetamine-type stimulants accounted for 60% of all positive drug tests in the June quarter, marking a year-on-year increase of 17.9%.
latest.details: More Australian workers are taking methamphetamine on the job, according to a report by the Drug Detection Agency. Amphetamine-type stimulants accounted for 60% of all positive drug tests in the June quarter, marking a year-on-year increase of 17.9%. Western Australia recorded the highest use of amphetamine-type stimulants at 88.6%, while other mainland states showed rates above 50%. Cocaine detections rose to 8% of positive tests, while cannabis was present in 39% of positive tests, though its usage slightly declined annually.
latest.full_brief: More Australian workers are taking methamphetamine on the job, according to a report by the Drug Detection Agency. Amphetamine-type stimulants accounted for 60% of all positive drug tests in the June quarter, marking a year-on-year increase of 17.9%. Western Australia recorded the highest use of amphetamine-type stimulants at 88.6%, while other mainland states showed rates above 50%. Cocaine detections rose to 8% of positive tests, while cannabis was present in 39% of positive tests, though its usage slightly declined annually.

brief_methodology.pl: Komentarz powstaje wyłącznie z przeczytanego tekstu artykułu. Musi przejść niezależny przegląd AI oraz pełną kontrolę językową; surowy tekst artykułu lub RSS nie może być komentarzem awaryjnym.
brief_methodology.en: A comment is created only from the retrieved article text. It must pass an independent AI review and the complete language gate; raw article or RSS text is never a fallback comment.

comment_quality_gate.pl: Publikowany jest wyłącznie komentarz utworzony z tekstu artykułu, zatwierdzony w niezależnym przeglądzie AI i przyjęty w całości przez rygorystyczną kontrolę językową. Jedno wadliwe zdanie odrzuca cały komentarz.
comment_quality_gate.en: Only an article-derived comment approved by an independent AI review and accepted in full by the strict language gate is published. One defective sentence rejects the whole comment.

rejected_examples.title: Iran threatens to block more trade routes as US launches fresh strikes
More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
Air India crash report likely to be ready in October, say investigators
More than 2,700 people may have died in exceptional May and June heatwaves in England and Wales
World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says

same_story_dedupe.rule: One underlying event gets one homepage card, even if multiple publishers cover it with different links.
